docs.text: Home Cluster ¶ This repository is my home Kubernetes cluster in a declarative state. Flux watches my cluster folder and makes the changes to my cluster based on the YAML manifests. This repository is built off the k8s-at-home/template-cluster-k3s repository. Cluster setup ¶ My cluster is k3s provisioned overtop Raspbian Lite using the K3Sup Cluster components ¶ cert-manager : Configured to create TLS certs for all ingress services automatically using LetsEncrypt. Repository structure ¶ The Git repository contains the following directories under cluster and are ordered below by how Flux will apply them. base directory is the entrypoint to Flux crds directory contains custom resource definitions (CRDs) that need to exist globally in your cluster before anything else exists core directory (depends on crds ) are important infrastructure applications (grouped by namespace) that should never be pruned by Flux apps directory (depends on core ) is where your common applications (grouped by namespace) could be placed, Flux will prune resources here if they are not tracked by Git anymore ./cluster ├── ./apps ├── ./base ├── ./core └── ./crds Tools ¶ Tool Purpose direnv Sets environment variable based on present working directory go-task Makefiles except in YAML pre-commit Enforce code consistency and verifies no secrets are pushed stern Tail logs in Kubernetes Thanks ¶ A lot of inspiration for my cluster came from the people that have shared their clusters over at awesome-home-kubernetes
docs.title: Introduction

docs.text: This repository is my home Kubernetes cluster in a declarative state. Flux watches my cluster folder and makes the changes to my cluster based on the YAML manifests. This repository is built off the k8s-at-home/template-cluster-k3s repository.
docs.title: Home Cluster

docs.text: My cluster is k3s provisioned overtop Raspbian Lite using the K3Sup
docs.title: Cluster setup

docs.text: cert-manager : Configured to create TLS certs for all ingress services automatically using LetsEncrypt.
docs.title: Cluster components

docs.text: The Git repository contains the following directories under cluster and are ordered below by how Flux will apply them. base directory is the entrypoint to Flux crds directory contains custom resource definitions (CRDs) that need to exist globally in your cluster before anything else exists core directory (depends on crds ) are important infrastructure applications (grouped by namespace) that should never be pruned by Flux apps directory (depends on core ) is where your common applications (grouped by namespace) could be placed, Flux will prune resources here if they are not tracked by Git anymore ./cluster ├── ./apps ├── ./base ├── ./core └── ./crds
docs.title: Repository structure

docs.text: Tool Purpose direnv Sets environment variable based on present working directory go-task Makefiles except in YAML pre-commit Enforce code consistency and verifies no secrets are pushed stern Tail logs in Kubernetes
docs.title: Tools

docs.text: A lot of inspiration for my cluster came from the people that have shared their clusters over at awesome-home-kubernetes
docs.title: Thanks

docs.text: Installing Kubernetes ¶ Update Ansible inventory configuration and run the k3s-install playbook Running a multi-master cluster with embedded etcd Create the first server k3sup install \ --ip <ip> \ --user <user> \ --ssh-key ~/.ssh/<key> \ --context homelab \ --local-path ~/.kube/config \ --merge \ --cluster \ --k3s-version v1.22.5+k3s1 \ --k3s-extra-args '--disable traefik --disable servicelb --disable metrics-server –flannel-backend=none' \ Join more servers k3sup join \ --ip <ip> \ --user <user> \ --ssh-key ~/.ssh/homelab \ --server \ --server-user <server_user> \ --server-ip <server_ip> \ --k3s-version v1.22.5+k3s1 \ --k3s-extra-args '--disable traefik --disable servicelb --disable metrics-server –flannel-backend=none' Join some other nodes k3sup join \ --ip <ip> \ --user <user> \ --ssh-key ~/.ssh/homelab \ --server-ip <server_ip> \ --k3s-version v1.22.5+k3s1 \ --k3s-extra-args '--disable traefik --disable servicelb --disable metrics-server –flannel-backend=none'
docs.title: Installing Kubernetes
